title: Cannot create unique constraint on char column
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test.keytest3 ( date_added INT NOT NULL, hash_key CHAR(32) NOT NULL, dimension1 INT NOT NULL, dimension2 INT NOT NULL, metric1 INT NOT NULL, metric2 INT NOT NULL, CONSTRAINT key_test3 UNIQUE (hash_key, date_added));
INSERT INTO test.keytest3 (date_added, hash_key, dimension1, dimension2, metric1, metric2) VALUES (1, 'abc', 1, 1, 2, 2);
INSERT INTO test.keytest3 (date_added, hash_key, dimension1, dimension2, metric1, metric2) VALUES (1, 'abc', 2, 2, 3, 3);
SELECT * FROM test.keytest3 WHERE hash_key IS NOT NULL;

CREATE TABLE test.keytest4 ( date_added INT NOT NULL, key INT NOT NULL, dimension1 INT NOT NULL, dimension2 INT NOT NULL, metric1 INT NOT NULL, metric2 INT NOT NULL, CONSTRAINT key_test4 UNIQUE (key, date_added));
key_bug_features: Creating unique constraint on char column fails
